name: Cadence Design Systems, Inc.
summary: Cadence Design Systems, Inc. (CDNS, Information Technology) — composite stance CONSTRUCTIVE. Latest reported quarter FY2026 Q1: revenue $1.47B (+18.7% YoY), net margin 22.8%. Valuation 50.0x forward P/E (+41% vs peers).
bull: Top-line momentum — revenue grew +18.7% YoY in FY2026 Q1 to $1.47B, with TTM revenue of $5.53B confirming the run-rate. Growth at this pace is unusual for a company of this scale and reflects either share gains, pricing power, or new-product cycles depending on the line-item mix in the Fundamental tab.

Profitability premium — net margin of 22.8% in the latest quarter sits well above the cross-sector ~10–15% benchmark, indicative of either structural pricing power or operating leverage from scale. Expect this to compress only modestly through-cycle.
Margin trajectory — net margin expanded +1.1pp over the last 4 quarters in the dataset. Management's cost-out commentary on recent calls is showing up in the P&L, not just the press release.
bear: Valuation premium — 50.0x forward P/E vs peer median of 35.5x — a 41% premium. The setup requires continued out-execution to maintain; any reversion to peer-median multiples compresses the stock meaningfully even before any earnings revision.
trigger_up: Revenue growth re-accelerates above 22% YoY on next print — confirms the demand environment hasn't degraded into the back half.
Net margin expands above 24.3% — confirms pricing power and/or cost discipline thesis.
Management raises FY guide on next quarterly call — direct upward revision to consensus.
trigger_down: Revenue growth decelerates below 16% YoY — signals demand has materially softened versus the latest disclosed run-rate.
Net margin compresses below 20.8% — flags either input-cost pressure or competitive dynamics shifting unfavorably.
Management cuts FY guide on next quarterly call — direct downward revision; historically sees 5–15% drawdown on print.
bottom_line: CONSTRUCTIVE. Cadence Design Systems, Inc. (CDNS) printed $1.47B in revenue in FY2026 Q1 (+18.7% YoY), net margin of 22.8%. At 50.0x forward P/E, the stock carries a 41% premium to peers (35.5x median) — the multiple needs continued execution to defend.A constructive view with limited near-term catalysts — set position size at half-conviction and add on weakness toward the 50-DMA or on a positive guidance revision.
